title: Experiment design considerations
description: Practice identifying examples of why randomization, blocking, blinding, and control are important in experiments.

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers recruited over $1{,}000$ patients with chronic back pain to participate in a study about the benefits of acupuncture. Each subject was randomly assigned to one of these treatment plans: traditional Chinese acupuncture, placebo acupuncture, and conventional pain treatment (medication and physical therapy).

**What is the primary purpose of randomly assigning subjects to the different treatment plans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To reduce the impact of sampling variability
To control against nonresponse
To eliminate confounding variables
To create groups that are as similar as possible so the main difference between the groups is the treatments
To ensure both the subjects and researchers are blind to who is in which group

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers working on a vaccine for HIV recruited over $16{,}000$ volunteers for a study. Each volunteer was randomly assigned to receive monthly injections of either the vaccine or a placebo for a year. The researchers were curious if subjects who received the vaccine would be less likely to contract HIV than subjects who received the placebo.

**What is the main reason for randomly assigning the subjects?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To reduce the impact of sampling variability
To control against nonresponse
To identify confounding variables
To create groups that are as similar as possible so the main difference between the groups is the treatment
To ensure both the subjects and researchers are blind to who is in which group

assessment_items.question_content:
A team of psychologists was curious if omega-3 fatty acid supplements could help treat depression in adolescents. They recruited $50$ adolescents with varying levels of depression to participate in an experiment. The researchers randomly assigned each subject to take either an omega-3 supplement or a placebo daily, and they monitored subjects' depression levels throughout the study.

**What is the main reason for randomly assigning the subjects?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To reduce the impact of sampling variability
To control against nonresponse
To identify confounding variables
To create groups that are as similar as possible so the main difference between the groups is the treatment
To ensure both the subjects and researchers are blind to who is in which group

assessment_items.question_content:
A company makes a bracelet that is designed to repel mosquitos so people don't have to wear bug spray. Researchers at the company are designing an experiment where volunteers will be assigned either the bracelet or bug spray to use at their homes. The researchers want to use a randomized block design.

**How should the researchers form the blocks?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Blocking happens after sample selection and before assignment to treatment groups.

Members of each block should be as similar as possible, based on other variables (not including treatment group) that might influence the outcome of the experiment.

What variables other than the treatment could influence how many mosquitos a volunteer encounters?
Volunteers within each block should live in similar environments to each other when it comes to mosquitos.

choices.content: Each volunteer should be randomly assigned to a block.
Volunteers in one block should use the bracelet, while volunteers in the other block should use bug spray.
Both the volunteers and researchers should be kept blind as to which block the volunteers are in.
Volunteers within each block should live in similar environments to each other when it comes to mosquitos.
Volunteers within each block should live in different environments from each other when it comes to mosquitos.

assessment_items.question_content:
Malaria is a disease spread by mosquitos. Researchers want to test a potential vaccine for the disease, so they plan on recruiting thousands of subjects from each of the $10$ countries that are most affected by the disease. The researchers want to use a randomized block design where some subjects receive the vaccine and the rest receive a placebo.

**How should the researchers form the blocks?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Blocking happens after sample selection and before assignment to treatment groups.

Members of each block should be as similar as possible, based on other variables (not including treatment group) that might influence the outcome of the experiment.

What variables other than the treatment could influence exposure to malaria?
Subjects within each block should live in the same country as each other.

choices.content: Each subject should be randomly assigned to a block.
Subjects in one block should get the vaccine, while subjects in the other block should get the placebo.
Both the subjects and researchers should be kept blind as to which block the subjects are in.
Subjects within each block should live in the same country as each other.
Subjects within each block should all live in different countries from each other.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sadie is redesigning her company's website. She wants to carry out an experiment that involves randomly assigning visitors to either the existing design or the new design. A user's experience is slightly different depending on what type of device (mobile or desktop) they use, so Sadie wants to use a randomized block design.

**How should Sadie form the blocks?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Blocking happens after sample selection and before assignment to treatment groups.

Members of each block should be as similar as possible, based on other variables (not including treatment group) that might influence the outcome of the experiment.

What variables other than the treatment could influence a user's experience?
Users within each block should use the same type of device as each other.

choices.content: Each user should be randomly assigned to a block.
Users in one block should see the new design, while subjects in the other block should see the existing design.
Both Sadie and the users should be kept blind as to which block the users are in.
Users within each block should use the same type of device as each other.
Users within each block should all use different types of devices from each other.

assessment_items.question_content:
An experiment involving a new pain relief medication involves subjects being randomly assigned to one of two treatment groups. Subjects in one group will take a daily dose of the new medication, and subjects in the other group will take an existing common pain medication. All subjects will check in with a doctor every week to report their symptoms and any side-effects.

**What does it mean for this experiment to be double-blind?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Blinding is when subjects don't know if they are in a treatment group or a control group. In a double-blind study, the subjects and those who collect the data are both unaware of who is in which group.
This experiment is double-blind if both the subjects and the doctors are unaware of who is taking which medication.

choices.content: Subjects in both treatment groups are unaware of who is taking which medication.
Both the subjects and the doctors are unaware of who is taking which medication.
Subjects in both treatment groups are unaware of what is being measured in the experiment.
Both the subjects and the doctors are unaware of what is being measured in the experiment.
The new and existing medications will both have unpredictable levels of pain relief and side-effects.

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers working on a vaccine for HIV recruited over $16{,}000$ volunteers for an experiment. Each volunteer was randomly assigned to receive monthly injections of either the vaccine or a placebo for a year. The researchers were curious if subjects who received the vaccine would be less likely to contract HIV than subjects who received the placebo.

**What does it mean for this experiment to be double-blind?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Blinding is when subjects don't know if they are in a treatment group or a control group. In a double-blind study, the subjects and those who collect the data are both unaware of who is in which group.
This experiment is double-blind if both the subjects and the researchers are unaware of who is receiving the vaccine and who is receiving the placebo.

choices.content: Subjects in both groups are unaware of who is receiving the vaccine and who is receiving the placebo.
Both the subjects and the researchers are unaware of who is receiving the vaccine and who is receiving the placebo.
Subjects in both groups are unaware of what is being measured in the experiment.
Both the subjects and the doctors are unaware of what is being measured in the experiment.
The vaccine and the placebo will both have unpredictable success rates and side-effects.

assessment_items.question_content:
A surgeon developed a new procedure for knee replacement surgery. To study its effectiveness, they recruit patients who need knee replacement surgery and are willing to participate in a blind study. The surgeon randomly assigns each volunteer to receive either the new procedure or the standard procedure. After the surgeon performs the procedure, they monitor each patient to see if one group has better results than the other.

**What does it mean for this experiment to be blind?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Blinding is when subjects don't know if they are in a treatment group or a control group. In a double-blind study, the subjects and those who collect the data are both unaware of who is in which group.
This experiment is blind if the volunteers are unaware of which surgery they receive.

choices.content: The volunteers are unaware of which surgery they receive.
The surgeon is unaware of which surgery each volunteer receives.
The volunteers are unaware of what is being measured in the experiment.
The surgeon is unaware of what is being measured in the experiment.
The new surgery will have unpredictable success and side-effects.

assessment_items.question_content:
A car manufacturer created a new voice-control system that allows drivers to have limited use of their phones while keeping both hands on the wheel. They want to know if the system results in drivers making fewer mistakes, so they recruit volunteers for an experiment.

Each subject is randomly assigned to one of three groups: voice-controlled phone use, regular phone use, or no phone use at all. Subjects drive the same simulated course while researchers send notifications at set moments to the drivers who can use phones. The researchers will then compare how many mistakes are made between the groups.

**What is the primary purpose of including a group who doesn't use a phone at all?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To serve as a control group for comparison
To utilize a randomized blocked design
To ensure subjects are blind as to which treatment group they are in
To reduce the impact of the placebo effect
To form a matched pair with the group that uses voice-control

assessment_items.question_content:
A company makes a bracelet containing magnets that they claim improves balance and overall athletic performance. Researchers recruit volunteers for an experiment. The subjects will test the effectiveness of the bracelet by performing a variety of athletic challenges with and without the bracelet.

Each subject will do each challenge $3$ times: wearing the actual bracelet, wearing a placebo bracelet, and wearing no bracelet at all. The order will be randomized for each activity, subjects won't know which bracelet is which.

**Why did the researchers include the scenario where subjects wear no bracelet at all?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To serve as a control group for comparison
To utilize a randomized blocked design
To correct for using volunteers rather than a random sample.
To control against nonresponse
To utilize a random cluster design

assessment_items.question_content:
A psychologist thinks that meditation might be a useful tool for patients trying to lose weight. They design an experiment and recruit volunteers who are all interested in losing weight. Half of the subjects (determined at random) will attend weekly guided meditation sessions and receive access to a diet and exercise program. The other half of the subjects will just have access to the diet and exercise program.

**What is the primary purpose of including a group who doesn't attend meditation sessions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To serve as a control group for comparison
To utilize a randomized blocked design
To ensure subjects are blind as to which treatment group they are in
To reduce the impact of the placebo effect
To form a matched pair with the group that attends meditation sessions
